content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://philpapers.org/rec/NOUTAT", "source_title": "The Alignment Tax: How Safety Training Creates Operationally Lethal AI Systems", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "This paper demonstrates that contemporary AI safety training paradigms, especially those centered on RLHF (Reinforcement Learning with Human...", "direct_quote": "This paper demonstrates that contemporary AI safety training paradigms, especially those centered on RLHF (Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback) and human-preference modelling, produce large language models that are systematically unreliable fo", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Through a four-layered analysis (Accessible, Philosophical, Formal, and Operational), the paper shows that these systems suppress epistemic ...", "direct_quote": "Through a four-layered analysis (Accessible, Philosophical, Formal, and Operational), the paper shows that these systems suppress epistemic risk signals by design, structurally biasing outputs toward pacified or downplayed interpretations.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "Drawing on phenomenology, control theory, and formal impossibility arguments, the paper proves that alignment as currently conceived cannot ...", "direct_quote": "Drawing on phenomenology, control theory, and formal impossibility arguments, the paper proves that alignment as currently conceived cannot produce safe or reliable AI systems.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
